chapter_context.summary: Records Daniel's visionary dream of four massive beasts rising from the windswept sea (lion, bear, leopard, and a terrifying ten-horned beast with a little speaking horn), followed by the grand heavenly court scene where the Ancient of Days bestows eternal dominion upon the Son of Man.

outline.text: Vision of the Four Great Beasts (Lion, Bear, Leopard, Dreadful Beast) and Ten Horns
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Vision of the Four Great Beasts (Lion, Bear, Leopard, Dreadful Beast) and Ten Horns (7:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Heavenly Court: The Ancient of Days and Coronation of the Son of Man
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Heavenly Court: The Ancient of Days and Coronation of the Son of Man (7:9-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Interpretation: Succession of Kings and the War of the Little Horn Against Saints
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Interpretation: Succession of Kings and the War of the Little Horn Against Saints (7:15-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 7, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
